forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- wballard
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: f980
+title: Record validator agent sessions to disk via RecordingAgent (audit trail + replay fixtures)
+---
+Wrap each validator's `Agent` in `agent_client_protocol_extras::RecordingAgent` so every validator run produces a structured transcript on disk. Re-use those transcripts as `PlaybackAgent` fixtures for end-to-end regression tests of the validator pipeline. One mechanism, two payoffs: clean audit trail and replay-based testing.
+
+## Why one task, not two
+
+I originally wanted to file (a) "structure the .avp log so prompt content isn't tangled with tracing lines" and (b) "add an automated end-to-end test for the validator pipeline." The transcript primitive in `agent-client-protocol-extras::RecordingAgent` solves both at once:
+
+- It already captures every Agent call (`initialize` / `new_session` / `prompt`) with its request, response, and the notifications streamed during the call (see `agent-client-protocol-extras/src/recording.rs::RecordedCall`).
+- The companion `PlaybackAgent` (in `agent-client-protocol-extras/src/playback.rs`) replays those recordings, letting tests run the full validator pipeline against frozen agent transcripts without the cost or flakiness of a real model.
+
+If we build a separate logging mechanism for #2 and a separate fixture mechanism for #3, we'll have two systems doing the same thing badly. Use the existing one twice.
+
+## What to change
+
+### 1. Wrap the validator agent in RecordingAgent
+
+In `avp-common/src/context.rs::agent` (the lazy agent constructor for validator execution), when a recording-output path is configured, wrap the inner agent: `RecordingAgent::new(inner_agent, path)`. The wrapped agent should be transparent to all existing callers (`ValidatorRunner`, the chain links) â€” `RecordingAgent` already implements the `Agent` trait by delegating to the wrapped agent.
+
+Configuration:
+- A flag in `.avp/avp.yaml` or an env var (`AVP_RECORD_VALIDATORS=1`, or a path like `AVP_RECORD_DIR=...`) gates recording. Default: off in production, on in `avp doctor`/test mode and when the user explicitly enables it.
+- Recording path layout: `.avp/recordings/<session_id>/<hook_type>/<ruleset>/<rule>.json`. Keep the directory under `.avp/` so the existing gitignore catches it.
+
+### 2. Don't use it as a replacement for tracing
+
+Tracing logs (`.avp/log`) stay as they are: cross-cutting operational events (hook decisions, rate limits, errors). The recording path is the per-rule conversational record â€” request/response/notifications â€” at the agent layer. Different audiences, different files. Don't try to merge them.
+
+### 3. Use recordings as test fixtures
+
+In `avp-common/tests/`, add an integration test that:
+
+1. Loads a checked-in recording fixture (a real qwen Stop-hook validation run, captured once).
+2. Constructs a `PlaybackAgent` from the recording.
+3. Runs the validator pipeline (`ValidatorRunner::execute_ruleset`) against that playback agent.
+4. Asserts on the produced `ExecutedRuleSet` â€” rule names, pass/fail status, message content.
+
+Check at least one fixture per failure mode we've seen:
+- Clean pass (rule converges on `{"status": "passed"}`).
+- Clean fail (rule converges on `{"status": "failed"}` with a specific finding).
+- Tool-call exchange (rule asks to read a file, agent returns content, rule decides). Demonstrates the per-rule agent loop is being exercised end-to-end.
+- Unparseable response (agent returns `<think>` block + JSON in markdown fences). Locks in the behavior chosen by `01KQ35V5GTDS4ED3VWG8SAH4DQ` (fail, not silent pass).
+
+### 4. Capture one canonical fixture corpus
+
+After tasks `01KQ34TAVZNR7FBYKNFSH0F19T`, `01KQ35MHFJQPMEKQ08PZKBKFY0`, and `01KQ35KFJXJ70GNB4ZPRJD6R43` land, do one manual qwen Stop-hook test run with `AVP_RECORD_VALIDATORS=1`, copy a representative subset of recordings into `avp-common/tests/fixtures/`, and use those as the seed corpus. Don't generate fixtures by hand â€” record real runs.
+
+## Acceptance
+
+- With recording enabled, a Stop-hook run produces one JSON file per (RuleSet, rule) under `.avp/recordings/<session_id>/.../`. Each file is a valid `RecordedSession` (per the existing `RecordingAgent::save` format) with `calls[].method`, `calls[].request`, `calls[].response`, `calls[].notifications` populated.
+- With recording disabled (default), no recordings are written. Validator pipeline behavior is unchanged.
+- An integration test in `avp-common/tests/` loads at least one fixture, runs the validator pipeline against `PlaybackAgent`, and asserts the right `ExecutedRuleSet` is produced. The test is hermetic â€” no network, no real model, deterministic.
+- The fixture directory has a short README explaining how to regenerate (`AVP_RECORD_VALIDATORS=1 avp ...`) so the corpus stays maintainable.
+- `cargo test -p avp-common` and `cargo clippy -p avp-common --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
+
+## Pairs with
+
+- Log this task as **does not block** the others. The validator changes (`01KQ34TAVZNR7FBYKNFSH0F19T`, `01KQ35MHFJQPMEKQ08PZKBKFY0`, `01KQ35KFJXJ70GNB4ZPRJD6R43`, `01KQ35V5GTDS4ED3VWG8SAH4DQ`) can land first, and this task captures the recordings that prove they work.
+- It's reasonable to land RecordingAgent integration *before* the others if it makes the others easier to verify â€” recordings of the current broken behavior are also valuable as regression-prevention fixtures.
+
+## Notes on what NOT to do
+
+- Don't invent a new transcript format. `RecordedCall` already exists with notifications support.
+- Don't merge recording into `.avp/log`. Different consumers, different lifecycles, different formats.
+- Don't try to make recording always-on by default. It writes a lot of data; the user should opt in (e.g. when debugging).
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Record validator agent sessions to disk via RecordingAgent (audit trail + replay fixtures)

Wrap each validator's `Agent` in `agent_client_protocol_extras::RecordingAgent` so every validator run produces a structured transcript on disk. Re-use those transcripts as `PlaybackAgent` fixtures for end-to-end regression tests of the validator pipeline. One mechanism, two payoffs: clean audit trail and replay-based testing.

## Why one task, not two

I originally wanted to file (a) "structure the .avp log so prompt content isn't tangled with tracing lines" and (b) "add an automated end-to-end test for the validator pipeline." The transcript primitive in `agent-client-protocol-extras::RecordingAgent` solves both at once:

- It already captures every Agent call (`initialize` / `new_session` / `prompt`) with its request, response, and the notifications streamed during the call (see `agent-client-protocol-extras/src/recording.rs::RecordedCall`).
- The companion `PlaybackAgent` (in `agent-client-protocol-extras/src/playback.rs`) replays those recordings, letting tests run the full validator pipeline against frozen agent transcripts without the cost or flakiness of a real model.

If we build a separate logging mechanism for #2 and a separate fixture mechanism for #3, we'll have two systems doing the same thing badly. Use the existing one twice.

## What to change

### 1. Wrap the validator agent in RecordingAgent

In `avp-common/src/context.rs::agent` (the lazy agent constructor for validator execution), when a recording-output path is configured, wrap the inner agent: `RecordingAgent::new(inner_agent, path)`. The wrapped agent should be transparent to all existing callers (`ValidatorRunner`, the chain links) â€” `RecordingAgent` already implements the `Agent` trait by delegating to the wrapped agent.

Configuration:
- A flag in `.avp/avp.yaml` or an env var (`AVP_RECORD_VALIDATORS=1`, or a path like `AVP_RECORD_DIR=...`) gates recording. Default: off in production, on in `avp doctor`/test mode and when the user explicitly enables it.
- Recording path layout: `.avp/recordings/<session_id>/<hook_type>/<ruleset>/<rule>.json`. Keep the directory under `.avp/` so the existing gitignore catches it.

### 2. Don't use it as a replacement for tracing

Tracing logs (`.avp/log`) stay as they are: cross-cutting operational events (hook decisions, rate limits, errors). The recording path is the per-rule conversational record â€” request/response/notifications â€” at the agent layer. Different audiences, different files. Don't try to merge them.

### 3. Use recordings as test fixtures

In `avp-common/tests/`, add an integration test that:

1. Loads a checked-in recording fixture (a real qwen Stop-hook validation run, captured once).
2. Constructs a `PlaybackAgent` from the recording.
3. Runs the validator pipeline (`ValidatorRunner::execute_ruleset`) against that playback agent.
4. Asserts on the produced `ExecutedRuleSet` â€” rule names, pass/fail status, message content.

Check at least one fixture per failure mode we've seen:
- Clean pass (rule converges on `{"status": "passed"}`).
- Clean fail (rule converges on `{"status": "failed"}` with a specific finding).
- Tool-call exchange (rule asks to read a file, agent returns content, rule decides). Demonstrates the per-rule agent loop is being exercised end-to-end.
- Unparseable response (agent returns `<think>` block + JSON in markdown fences). Locks in the behavior chosen by `01KQ35V5GTDS4ED3VWG8SAH4DQ` (fail, not silent pass).

### 4. Capture one canonical fixture corpus

After tasks `01KQ34TAVZNR7FBYKNFSH0F19T`, `01KQ35MHFJQPMEKQ08PZKBKFY0`, and `01KQ35KFJXJ70GNB4ZPRJD6R43` land, do one manual qwen Stop-hook test run with `AVP_RECORD_VALIDATORS=1`, copy a representative subset of recordings into `avp-common/tests/fixtures/`, and use those as the seed corpus. Don't generate fixtures by hand â€” record real runs.

## Acceptance

- With recording enabled, a Stop-hook run produces one JSON file per (RuleSet, rule) under `.avp/recordings/<session_id>/.../`. Each file is a valid `RecordedSession` (per the existing `RecordingAgent::save` format) with `calls[].method`, `calls[].request`, `calls[].response`, `calls[].notifications` populated.
- With recording disabled (default), no recordings are written. Validator pipeline behavior is unchanged.
- An integration test in `avp-common/tests/` loads at least one fixture, runs the validator pipeline against `PlaybackAgent`, and asserts the right `ExecutedRuleSet` is produced. The test is hermetic â€” no network, no real model, deterministic.
- The fixture directory has a short README explaining how to regenerate (`AVP_RECORD_VALIDATORS=1 avp ...`) so the corpus stays maintainable.
- `cargo test -p avp-common` and `cargo clippy -p avp-common --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.

## Pairs with

- Log this task as **does not block** the others. The validator changes (`01KQ34TAVZNR7FBYKNFSH0F19T`, `01KQ35MHFJQPMEKQ08PZKBKFY0`, `01KQ35KFJXJ70GNB4ZPRJD6R43`, `01KQ35V5GTDS4ED3VWG8SAH4DQ`) can land first, and this task captures the recordings that prove they work.
- It's reasonable to land RecordingAgent integration *before* the others if it makes the others easier to verify â€” recordings of the current broken behavior are also valuable as regression-prevention fixtures.

## Notes on what NOT to do

- Don't invent a new transcript format. `RecordedCall` already exists with notifications support.
- Don't merge recording into `.avp/log`. Different consumers, different lifecycles, different formats.
- Don't try to make recording always-on by default. It writes a lot of data; the user should opt in (e.g. when debugging).
